RQMD-CORE-013 (domain/index sync maintenance) since it would reduce manual doc drift over time.

## README.md should be fully generated by domain files

This file should be a true index of the last time the tool ran.
Maybe even we should "assume that all updates to the markdown files are made via the tool."

## rqMD instead of rqMD?

I think rqMD is so nice and short! And it still conveys the right sound when you prononce it naturally even without the "e"!

## Rename Key feature?

Finally, Probably should also have a feature for easily changing the key for all files for quickly renaming from the default of "REQ-" to something more project specific. Add a new requirement, but don't

RQMD-PORTABILITY-009: graceful startup errors
RQMD-INTERACTIVE-011: preflight write-permission gate
RQMD-CORE-013: domain-sync maintenance over time
RQMD-PACKAGING-007: semantic versioning policy
RQMD-PACKAGING-006: PyPI metadata hardening
RQMD-PACKAGING-008: publish to PyPI on release
RQMD-PORTABILITY-010: strip/restore emoji commands
RQMD-PORTABILITY-007: status catalog config precedence
RQMD-INTERACTIVE-010: customizable status catalog/colors
RQMD-ROLLUP-005: global roll-up report mode
RQMD-SORTING-005: configurable sort strategies
RQMD-PORTABILITY-006: future config file defaults
If you want, I can start immediately with item 1 and carry it through code, tests, and requirement status updates in one pass.

### Round 2

I’ll rebuild it from live data by querying current proposed requirements via `uv run rqmd --filter-status proposed --json`, then prioritize by impact, risk reduction, and implementation leverage.

Ran terminal command:  uv run rqmd --filter-status proposed --json --no-interactive --no-summary-table

Excellent timing to refresh this. Based on the live proposed set from rqmd, here is a new prioritized queue optimized for development leverage, safety, and delivery sequencing.

1. RQMD-PORTABILITY-009: Graceful startup errors for docs availability and permissions  
Why first: highest user-facing reliability gain, low implementation risk, and improves every invocation path.

2. RQMD-INTERACTIVE-011: Preflight write-permission gate before interactive mode  
Why second: prevents destructive/frustrating interactive sessions and complements portability error handling.

3. RQMD-CORE-013: Domain-sync maintenance over time  
Why third: reduces long-term docs drift and maintenance overhead across the whole requirements system.

4. RQMD-PORTABILITY-010: One-time emoji strip and restore commands  
Why fourth: concrete operational value for teams and CI output preferences, with clear idempotency behavior to test.

5. RQMD-ROLLUP-005: Cross-file/global roll-up report mode  
Why fifth: immediate visibility win for planning and triage, especially once machine-readable output exists.

6. RQMD-PORTABILITY-007: Status customization config location and precedence  
Why sixth: unlocks team-level extensibility; best done after baseline reliability/error-path hardening above.

7. RQMD-INTERACTIVE-010: Customizable status catalog and colors  
Why seventh: strong UX value but should follow config precedence to avoid rework.

8. RQMD-SORTING-005: Configurable sort strategy catalog  
Why eighth: useful but less urgent than correctness/reliability/config foundations.

9. RQMD-PORTABILITY-006: Optional future config file support  
Why ninth: broad platform feature, but better once specific config-backed features are settled.

10. RQMD-PACKAGING-007: Semantic versioning policy  
Why tenth: important governance work, but not runtime behavior.

11. RQMD-PACKAGING-006: PyPI metadata hardening  
Why eleventh: release-readiness polish that can be done once behavior churn slows.

12. RQMD-PACKAGING-008: Publish to PyPI on GitHub release  
Why twelfth: should come last so automation reflects stabilized packaging and policy decisions.

Suggested execution waves:
1. Reliability wave: items 1 to 3  
2. Operator value wave: items 4 to 5  
3. Extensibility wave: items 6 to 9  
4. Release pipeline wave: items 10 to 12

If you want, I can start immediately with item 1 and carry it through code, tests, and status updates in one pass.
